summary:
FTMO is an educational and technology-driven company established in 2015, offering simulated trading environments through its structured evaluation process. FTMO now offers two evaluation products — FTMO Challenge: 2-Step and FTMO Challenge: 1-Step. All FTMO Challenge accounts are simulated accounts with fictitious funds, and trading is not in real financial instruments.

2-Step rewards start at 80%, with 90% available through the Scaling Plan. 1-Step pays 90% on the FTMO Account. Fees start from €89 for the $10,000 2-Step challenge, and traders can reach a maximum FTMO Account allocation of $400,000 before scaling, with the Scaling Plan growing up to $2,000,000.
pros: Four platform choices (MT4, MT5, cTrader, DXtrade)
bestFor: Traders who want either a 1-Step or 2-Step evaluation, automatic scaling, and access to a wide range of platforms and educational resources
notIdealFor: Traders who need higher initial account sizes beyond $200K before scaling, or those uncomfortable with the 1-Step trailing drawdown and Best Day Rule

verdictBlocks.heading: Final Verdict on FTMO
verdictBlocks.body:
FTMO offers two evaluation products — FTMO Challenge: 2-Step and FTMO Challenge: 1-Step — with fees starting from €89 for the $10,000 2-Step challenge. All FTMO Challenge accounts are simulated accounts with fictitious funds, and trading is not in real financial instruments. FTMO is an educational and technology-driven company, not a broker.

The 2-Step has a 10% then 5% profit target structure with 5% max daily loss and 10% max loss, both based on initial balance. The 1-Step has a 10% profit target with 3% max daily loss and a trailing 10% max loss based on the highest end-of-day balance or initial balance (whichever is higher), plus a Best Day Rule where the most profitable day cannot exceed 50% of Positive Days' Profit.

Rewards are processed on a bi-weekly basis, and traders can request a reward after 14 days from the first trade. 2-Step rewards start at 80% and scale to 90%; 1-Step rewards are 90% on the FTMO Account. The Scaling Plan requires a minimum 4-month cycle with at least 10% total net simulated profit and at least 2 rewards processed, growing accounts by 25% per scale-up to a maximum of $2,000,000. Current flexibility is based on 1-Step vs 2-Step, plus Standard vs Swing conditions where relevant.

faq.a: FTMO is an educational and technology-driven company established in 2015 in Prague, Czech Republic, with an Excellent Trustpilot rating of 4.8/5 with approximately 39,700 reviews. FTMO's 2025 company history mentions a strategic partnership with OANDA for US expansion. The firm provides clear rules and a structured evaluation process, with 24/7 customer support in 20 languages.

faq.q: How much is the FTMO payout?
faq.a: FTMO calls its payouts 'rewards.' For 2-Step, rewards start at 80% and can increase to 90% through the Scaling Plan. For 1-Step, the reward is 90% of simulated profits on the FTMO Account. Reward withdrawals are available after 14 days from the first trade, and FTMO processes rewards on a bi-weekly basis with an average processing time of 8 hours. The FTMO Challenge fee is a one-time refundable fee — you receive a 100% refund of your initial fee with your first reward withdrawal.

faq.q: What evaluation products does FTMO offer?
faq.a: FTMO offers two products: FTMO Challenge 2-Step (10% then 5% targets, 5% daily loss, 10% max loss, unlimited trading period, 4 minimum trading days per phase) and FTMO Challenge 1-Step (10% target, 3% daily loss, trailing 10% max loss, Best Day Rule, 4 minimum trading days). Account types include Standard and Swing, with Swing allowing overnight, weekend, and news holding.

Yes, FTMO processes rewards on a bi-weekly basis. Traders can request a reward after 14 days from the first trade. 2-Step rewards start at 80% and can reach 90% through the Scaling Plan. 1-Step rewards are 90% of simulated profits. All trading is conducted in a simulated environment with fictitious funds.
